title: Device request: Nedis Zigbee 2-Gang Dimmer (ZBWD20RD / TS110E)
excerpt:
Hi,

I would like to request support for a Zigbee dimmer device that is currently not properly recognized by Homey.


📦 Device Information
	•	Brand: Nedis
	•	Model: ZBWD20RD
	•	Type: 2-gang Zigbee dimmer (Triac)


🔍 Zigbee Fingerprint
	•	modelId: TS110E
	•	manufacturerName: _TZ3210_mt5xjoy6


🔌 Endpoint Structure

The device exposes two independent endpoints:
	•	Endpoint 1
	•	Clusters: onOff (0x0006), levelControl (0x0008)
	•	Endpoint 2
	•	Clusters: onOff (0x0006), levelControl (0x0008)


📡 Full Zigbee Interview (from Homey Developer Tools)

```
{
  "modelId": "TS110E",
  "manufacturerName": "_TZ3210_mt5xjoy6",
  "endpoints": [
    {
      "endpointId": 1,
      "inputClusters": [0,3,4,5,6,8,4096,57345],
      "outputClusters": [10,25]
    },
    {
      "endpointId": 2,
      "inputClusters": [4,5,6,8,57345],
      "outputClusters": []
    }
  ]
}
```

⚠️ Current Behavior in Homey
	•	Device is detected as a generic Zigbee dimmer
	•	Only one channel is exposed
	•	The second endpoint is not accessible
	•	Device is not mapped to a specific driver

🔘 Switch / Input Behavior
	•	External switches: momentary (spring return)
	•	Observed behavior:
	•	Holding button → turns off light
	•	Releasing button → state resets (light turns back on)

✅ Expected Behavior
	•	Two independent dimmer channels:
	•	Endpoint 1 → L1
	•	Endpoint 2 → L2
	•	Each should support:
	•	on/off
	•	dimming
	•	Proper handling of momentary switches:
	•	short press → toggle
	•	long press → dim

🧪 Tested Scenarios
	•	Tuya Zigbee app
	•	Generic Zigbee inclusion

Same result in all cases.

📌 Summary

This is a Tuya-based TS110E dual dimmer (Nedis ZBWD20RD).

The hardware clearly supports two endpoints, but Homey currently exposes only one.

🙏 Request

Could support be added by:
	•	Adding fingerprint:
	•	TS110E / _TZ3210_mt5xjoy6
	•	Mapping both endpoints as separate dimmers
	•	Fixing input handling for momentary switches

Thanks!
